info.description:
### Introduction

The public API of the Maqqie Platform makes it possible for third parties to integrate their applications with the Maqqie Platform.

The endpoints are grouped by:

* Sub domain: a specific and coherent sub set of entities of the Maqqie platform.
* Role: the allowed operations and the semantics of these operations differs per role. 

See the [glossary](glossary.html) for an explanation of the terms used in this API.

All endpoints require credentials. If you need access to the API, please send a request for an account to the [public API team](mailto:public-api-support@maqqie.nl). Once you have received the client credentials, access tokens can be requested at: [https://maqqie.app/auth/realms/maqqie/protocol/openid-connect/token](https://maqqie.app/auth/realms/maqqie/protocol/openid-connect/token)
info.title: Maqqie Platform Public API

get.description: Get existing signed and unsigned framework agreement (if any) for the given worker.

parameters.description: The unique id of the worker, as returned from the /create-worker endpoint.
parameters.name: workerId

400.description:
The request could not be processed. This could be due to a number of reasons:
* The request does not have all required data.
* Data in the request is invalid.
* Data in the request is inconsistent.
* The operation is not allowed given the current state of the data.

401.description: The request did not contain credentials identifying the caller, or the provided credentials were invalid.

403.description: The data that the request referred to is inaccessible to the caller.

get.summary: retrieve the info of a worker

post.description:
Register hours for 1 or more combinations of a shift and a worker. The hours will still have to be approved on the Maqqie platform by the hirer.

The type of the registered hours is based on the collective agreement (collectieve arbeidsovereenkomst, CAO) that applies to the worker and the shift. A single period being registered may contain multiple types, in which case the hours will be split into multiple periods.

All shifts must belong to the hirer that invokes this API, and a shift signup for each combination of shift and worker must exist.

post.description:
Register hours for 1 or more temporary work payroll placements. The hours will still have to be approved on the Maqqie platform by the hirer.

The type of the registered hours is based on the collective agreement (collectieve arbeidsovereenkomst, CAO) that applies to the temporary work payroll placement. A single period being registered may contain multiple types, in which case the hours will be split into multiple periods.

All temporary work payroll placements must belong to the hirer that invokes this API.

post.description:
Create a definition for a new temporary work payroll placement. The minimal information must be provided, and a number of optional fields may be passed.

The user ID instead of the worker ID is used to identify the worker. The user must be a worker.

**This is a legacy endpoint**. [create-temporary-work-payroll-placement-definition-minimal-v1](#/Temporary%20work%20payroll%20placements%2C%20for%20hirers/CreateTemporaryWorkPayrollPlacementDefinitionMinimalV1) should be used instead. Only use this if your system does not know the worker ID.

post.description:
Add 1 or more of the labels that the hirer has defined for workers, to a worker, using the user ID instead of the worker ID. The user must be a worker.

The labels do not have to exist already. If the label is unknown, it will be created.

If a label has already been added to a worker, this will not result in an error.

**This is a legacy endpoint.** [add-hirer-labels-to-worker-v1](#/Worker%20profiles%2C%20for%20hirers/AddHirerLabelsToWorkerV1) should be used instead. Only use this if your system does not know the worker ID.

post.description:
Remove 1 or more of the labels that the hirer has defined for workers, from a worker, using the user ID instead of the worker ID. The user must be a worker.

If the worker already does not have the label assigned to them, this will not result in an error.

**This is a legacy endpoint.** [remove-hirer-labels-from-worker-v1](#/Worker%20profiles%2C%20for%20hirers/RemoveHirerLabelsFromWorkerV1) should be used instead. Only use this if your system does not know the worker ID.

hoursRegistrations.description:
The hours to be registered for shift-worker combinations (shift signups).

Both shifts and workers may occur multiple times. Multiple combinations for the same shift and worker are also allowed.
hoursRegistrations.title: The periods that workers worked on shifts to be registered.

allOf.description:
A single period when a worker worked on a shift.

The period may contain 1 or more breaks. The total duration of these breaks must be given.

Maqqie will assume the worker had only 1 break half way through the worked period. This assumption could result in incorrect hour types being used for (some parts of) the worked period. To ensure the correct hour types are always used, split an hours registration into multiple hours registrations. Use 1 period for each period before, between and after the breaks, and set the break duration to 0 minutes.

shiftId.description:
The ID of the shift for which to register hours.

This shift must belong to the hirer that invokes this API. There must be a shift signup for this shift and the worker.
shiftId.title: The ID of the shift for which to register hours.

workerId.description:
The ID of the worker for which to register hours.

There must be a shift signup for the shift and this worker.
workerId.title: The ID of the worker for which to register hours.

deduplicationId.description:
Clients (a specific account) are allowed to generate a unique ID for each worked period, and send this ID with the period. If the ID already exists in the database (implying that the period has already been registered), the period will not be registered again.

Note that there is no check to see if the data sent with a deduplication ID matches the data already in our database. If the ID matches, it is assumed that the data sent with the ID is the same as the data already in the database.
deduplicationId.title: A client-specific ID that can be used to prevent duplication insertion of the hours.

hoursRegistrations.description:
The hours to be registered for temporary work payroll placements.

The same temporary work payroll placement may occur multiple times.
hoursRegistrations.title: The periods that workers worked for temporary work payroll placements to be registered.

allOf.description:
A single period when a worker worked for a temporary work payroll placement.

The period may contain 1 or more breaks. The total duration of these breaks must be given.

Maqqie will assume the worker had only 1 break half way through the worked period. This assumption could result in incorrect hour types being used for (some parts of) the worked period. To ensure the correct hour types are always used, split an hours registration into multiple hours registrations. Use 1 period for each period before, between and after the breaks, and set the break duration to 0 minutes.

temporaryWorkPayrollPlacementId.description:
The ID of the temporary work payroll placement for which to register hours.

This temporary work payroll placement must belong to the hirer that invokes this API.
temporaryWorkPayrollPlacementId.title: The ID of the temporary work payroll placement for which to register hours.

deduplicationId.description:
Clients (a specific account) are allowed to generate a unique ID for each worked period, and send this ID with the period. If the ID already exists in the database (implying that the period has already been registered), the period will not be registered again.

Note that there is no check to see if the data sent with a deduplication ID matches the data already in our database. If the ID matches, it is assumed that the data sent with the ID is the same as the data already in the database.
deduplicationId.title: A client-specific ID that can be used to prevent duplication insertion of the hours.

startTimestamp.description:
The start timestamp must be earlier than the end timestamp.

This field, the end timestamp and the break duration are used to calculate the actual hours that the worker worked: end timestamp - start timestamp - break duration.
startTimestamp.title: The time at which the worker started working.

endTimestamp.description:
The end timestamp must be later than the start timestamp.

This field, the start timestamp and the break duration are used to calculate the actual hours that the worker worked: end timestamp - start timestamp - break duration.
endTimestamp.title: The time at which the worker stopped working.

breakDurationInMinutes.description:
The total number of minutes the worker had breaks between the start and end timestamps. This duration must be less than the number of minutes between the start and end timestamps.

This field, the start timestamp and the end timestamp are used to calculate the actual hours that the worker worked: end timestamp - start timestamp - break duration.
breakDurationInMinutes.title: The total duration of all breaks (in minutes).

allOf.description: The string is empty or only contains whitespace characters.

field.description: The path to the field containing the string that is blank.
field.title: The field path.

allOf.description: The text does not have the correct format.

field.description: The path to the field containing the text not having the correct format.
field.title: The field path.

UnknownErrorV1.description:
An error occurred, but a corresponding error has not been defined in the API yet.

The API team has been notified, and the API will be extended with an error type for the error that occurred.

allOf.description: The worker has no existing placement with the hirer, has not used the gate of the hirer, and is not following the hirer.

field.description: The path to the field containing the identifier of the worker.
field.title: The field path.

FrameworkAgreementGetResponseV1.description:
If there is a signed agreement, `unsignedAgreement` is `null`.
If there is no signed agreement, but there is an unsigned agreement, `signedAgreement` is `null`.
If neither exists, both are `null`.

FrameworkAgreementSignedEventV1.description: Request body for the webhook that is called once a worker has signed the framework agreement.

documentId.description:
The id of the document containing the signed framework agreement.
The document can be retrieved using the /worker-document endpoint.

signingUrl.description: A URL to the web UI (Stiply) that shows the agreement to the worker and allows them to e-sign it.

signingUrlExpirationTimestamp.description:
The timestamp (UTC) at which the signing URL will expire.
When browsing to the URL after this timestamp, the web UI (Stiply) will show an error page.

workerId.description: The unique id of the worker, as returned from the /create-worker endpoint.

isPayrollTaxReduction.description: true if the worker wants us to apply payroll tax reduction (Dutch: loonheffingskorting), false otherwise

payrollTaxReductionStartDate.description:
The date from which the payroll tax reduction should be applied. 
Required if isPayrollTaxReduction is true, ignored otherwise.

firstName.description: The first name of the worker.

middleName.description: The middle name of the worker.

lastName.description: The last name of the worker.

emailAddress.description: The email address of the worker.

mobileNumber.description: The mobile number of the worker.

firstNameFormal.description: The formal first name of the worker.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the worker.

placeOfBirth.description: The place of birth of the worker.

nationality.description: The country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

identificationBy.description: The method used for worker identification.

idCheckDate.description: The date when the identification document was checked.

CountryCode.description: The country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

street.description: The street name of the address.

houseNumber.description: The house number of the address.

houseNumberAddition.description: The addition to the house number (if any).

zipCode.description: The postal code (ZIP) of the address.

country.description: The country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

mobileNumber.description: The mobile number of the worker.

bsnDocument.description: The base64-encoded image of the bsn document.

base64DocumentBack.description: The base64-encoded image of the back of the identity document.

base64DocumentFront.description: The base64-encoded image of the front of the identity document.

documentExpiryDate.description: The expiration date of the identification document.

documentNationality.description: The country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

tags.description: Information about, setting up and finalizing placements of workers at a hirer under temporary work payroll.
tags.name: Temporary work payroll placements, for hirers

tags.description:
Retrieval, registration and (dis)approval  of hours and expenses.

Registration of hours and expenses must be done using these endpoints in the following cases:

* The hirer instead of the worker registers the hours.
* The hours and expenses were registered by workers in another system, and the hirer approved them there.
tags.name: Hours and expenses, for hirers

tags.description: Access to and updating of the profiles of workers.
tags.name: Worker profiles, for hirers
